cities.name: Kavaratti
cities.overview: Kavaratti is the administrative capital of Lakshadweep and one of the most developed islands in the archipelago. It offers pristine white-sand beaches, crystal-clear lagoons, vibrant coral reefs, and a perfect blend of laid-back island life with basic modern amenities.
cities.history: Kavaratti has been inhabited for centuries and became the capital when Lakshadweep was declared a Union Territory in 1956. The island is home to the famous Ujra Mosque and serves as the main hub for government and tourism activities.

places.name: Kavaratti Beach
places.description: One of the most beautiful beaches in Lakshadweep with powdery white sand, turquoise lagoon waters, and excellent snorkeling opportunities right off the shore.
places.tips: Snorkeling gear is available for rent. Visit early morning for the calmest waters and best visibility. Carry reef-safe sunscreen.

places.name: Ujra Mosque
places.description: The most famous mosque in Lakshadweep, known for its unique architecture and intricate wood carvings. A peaceful spiritual landmark of the island.
places.tips: Dress modestly and remove shoes before entering. Non-Muslims can view the exterior and surrounding gardens.

places.name: Kavaratti Lagoon Snorkeling & Glass-bottom Boat
places.description: World-class snorkeling in the shallow lagoon teeming with colourful coral reefs, tropical fish, and marine life. Glass-bottom boat rides are also available.
places.tips: Book through licensed operators only. Life jackets are provided. Best time is morning when water visibility is highest.

cities.name: Agatti
cities.overview: Agatti is the only island in Lakshadweep with an airport, making it the primary entry point for tourists. It is renowned for its stunning lagoon, coral reefs, and relaxed island vibe.
cities.history: Agatti has been a fishing and coconut cultivation hub for centuries and became the aviation gateway when the airport was commissioned in 1988.

places.name: Agatti Beach
places.description: A pristine stretch of white coral sand fringed by coconut palms and a vibrant lagoon perfect for swimming and sunbathing.
places.tips: The lagoon is shallow and safe for swimming. Snorkeling right off the beach is excellent. Carry reef-safe sunscreen.

places.name: Agatti Lagoon
places.description: One of the most beautiful lagoons in the world, home to vibrant coral reefs and thousands of tropical fish. Ideal for snorkeling and glass-bottom boat rides.
places.tips: Book a full-day lagoon trip with lunch on a sandbank. Licensed operators provide equipment and guides.

places.name: Scuba Diving at Agatti
places.description: World-class scuba diving in pristine coral reefs with excellent visibility and diverse marine life including turtles, rays, and reef sharks.
places.tips: PADI-certified operators are available. No prior experience needed for discover scuba diving. Book in advance.

cities.name: Bangaram
cities.overview: Bangaram is a stunning uninhabited island (except for the resort) known for its pristine beaches, crystal-clear waters, and world-class diving and snorkeling.
cities.history: Once a coconut plantation island, Bangaram was developed into an eco-friendly resort in the 1980s and remains one of the most exclusive destinations in Lakshadweep.

places.name: Bangaram Beach
places.description: One of the most pristine and secluded beaches in India with powdery white sand and a calm turquoise lagoon perfect for swimming and relaxation.
places.tips: The beach is exclusive to resort guests. Sunset walks are magical. No day visitors are allowed on the island.

places.name: Bangaram Lagoon
places.description: A breathtaking lagoon surrounding the island with vibrant coral reefs and abundant marine life, ideal for snorkeling and diving.
places.tips: Resort organises snorkeling trips. The lagoon is shallow and safe for beginners. Marine life is rich year-round.

cities.name: Kadmat
cities.overview: Kadmat is famous for its 7-km long pristine beach and shallow lagoon, making it one of the best islands for swimming and water sports.
cities.history: Traditionally a coconut and fishing island, Kadmat has been developed for eco-tourism with a government resort.

places.name: Kadmat Beach
places.description: A spectacular 7-km long stretch of white sand beach with a calm, shallow lagoon on one side and the open sea on the other.
places.tips: Perfect for long walks and swimming. The lagoon is safe and ideal for families. Resort organises water sports.

places.name: Kadmat Lagoon
places.description: Shallow lagoon with excellent snorkeling and coral reefs just a short distance from the shore.
places.tips: Snorkeling is superb here. Glass-bottom boat rides are available. Wear reef-safe sunscreen.

cities.name: Kalpeni
cities.overview: Kalpeni is known for its stunning lagoons, multiple small islands, and rich marine biodiversity. It offers a very peaceful and authentic island experience.
cities.history: Kalpeni has a rich history of coconut cultivation and traditional island life. It was one of the first islands opened for tourism.

places.name: Kalpeni Lagoon
places.description: A vast and beautiful lagoon with multiple small islands (called 'bods') inside it, offering superb snorkeling and kayaking.
places.tips: Boat trips to the small islands inside the lagoon are highly recommended. The water is exceptionally clear.

cities.name: Minicoy
cities.overview: Minicoy is the southernmost and largest island in Lakshadweep, famous for its unique Maldivian-style culture, the iconic lighthouse, and beautiful lagoons.
cities.history: Minicoy has strong cultural ties with the Maldives due to its proximity and shared history. The island has its own unique language and traditions.

places.name: Minicoy Lighthouse
places.description: The tallest lighthouse in Lakshadweep, offering panoramic views of the island and surrounding sea from the top.
places.tips: Climb the 105 steps for stunning views. Best visited in the morning. Photography is allowed from the top.

places.name: Minicoy Lagoon
places.description: A massive lagoon with excellent snorkeling and the famous Viringili sandbank, a small uninhabited island inside the lagoon.
places.tips: Boat trips to Viringili sandbank are a highlight. The lagoon is perfect for swimming and water sports.

cities.name: Andrott
cities.overview: Andrott is the largest inhabited island in Lakshadweep and is known for its strong Islamic heritage and the tomb of Saint Ubaidullah.
cities.history: Andrott is believed to be the first island in Lakshadweep where Islam was introduced in the 7th century by Saint Ubaidullah.

places.name: Tomb of Saint Ubaidullah
places.description: The tomb of the saint who brought Islam to Lakshadweep in the 7th century. A revered pilgrimage site for the islanders.
places.tips: Dress modestly. The surrounding area is peaceful and offers a glimpse into local culture.

cities.name: Suheli
cities.overview: Suheli is a remote coral atoll consisting of three small islands known for its untouched beauty, rich marine life, and excellent diving opportunities.
cities.history: Traditionally used for coconut cultivation, Suheli is one of the least visited islands and offers a true off-the-grid experience.

places.name: Suheli Lagoon
places.description: A pristine lagoon with exceptional coral reefs and marine biodiversity, ideal for advanced snorkeling and diving.
places.tips: Only accessible by live-aboard or special permits. The marine life is among the best in Lakshadweep.
